questions.question: Why do you want to work at IBM?
questions.answer: Explain your interest in IBM's innovation, hybrid cloud, AI technologies, research culture, and career growth opportunities.

questions.question: Tell me about a project where you solved a business problem using technology.
questions.answer: Use the STAR method to explain the problem, your solution, technologies used, and measurable impact.

questions.question: Describe a time you worked with a diverse team.
questions.answer: Explain how you collaborated, communicated effectively, and achieved the project goals.

questions.question: Describe the most challenging technical problem you have solved.
questions.answer: Explain your analysis, approach, implementation, and final outcome.

questions.question: How do you keep yourself updated with new technologies?
questions.answer: Mention certifications, documentation, technical blogs, research papers, and hands-on projects.

questions.question: Tell me about a time you took ownership of a project.
questions.answer: Describe how you identified a problem, took responsibility, and delivered results.

questions.question: Describe a time you had to quickly adapt to a new technology.
questions.answer: Explain your learning process and how you successfully applied the new technology.

questions.question: How do you explain technical concepts to business stakeholders?
questions.answer: Use simple language, real-world examples, and focus on business value.

questions.question: How do you ensure software quality in your projects?
questions.answer: Discuss testing, documentation, code reviews, and continuous improvement.

questions.question: Why should IBM hire you?
questions.answer: Highlight your technical expertise, teamwork, problem-solving skills, and willingness to learn.
